skill.name: firecrawl-parse
skill.description: Extract and convert local documents (PDF, DOCX, DOC, ODT, RTF, XLSX, XLS, HTML) into clean, well-formatted markdown saved to disk. Supports AI-powered summaries and question-answering over parsed content.
skill.summary: Convert local files like PDFs and DOCX into clean markdown using the Firecrawl CLI parse command.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The 22 'backtick execution' flags are markdown formatting in a documentation file, not actual shell commands. The 'path traversal' flag on line 61 is a relative link in markdown text. The two 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags match DOCX/DOC file extensions in natural language descriptions, not crypto code. The skill only invokes the scoped `firecrawl` and `npx firecrawl` CLI commands and is a safe documentation wrapper.

low_findings.title: Shell command invocation via scoped Bash tool
low_findings.description: The skill uses `allowed-tools` to restrict execution to `Bash(firecrawl *)` and `Bash(npx firecrawl *)`, which is a safe scoping pattern. The flagged backtick locations are all markdown-formatted documentation examples, not executable code injection vectors. Acceptable for a documentation skill.

content.user_title: Parse any local document into clean markdown
content.value_statement: Manually extracting text from PDFs and DOCX files is slow and error-prone. This skill uses the Firecrawl CLI to convert local files into well-formatted markdown saved to disk for easy downstream processing.
actual_capabilities: Convert PDF, DOCX, DOC, ODT, RTF, XLSX, XLS, and HTML files to clean markdown.
Save parsed output directly to disk using the `-o` output flag.
Generate AI-powered summaries of parsed documents with the `-S` flag.
Answer questions about parsed document content using the `-Q` flag.
Support a 50 MB maximum upload size per file.
Work with scoped Bash permissions restricted to firecrawl CLI commands only.
limitations: Requires a Firecrawl account and API key configured locally.
Consumes credits per page (approximately 1 credit per PDF page).
Maximum file size is 50 MB per upload.
Only handles local files; URLs should use the `firecrawl-scrape` skill instead.

use_cases.title: Extract text from research PDFs
use_cases.description: Convert academic papers and research PDFs into markdown for analysis, summarization, or citation extraction.

use_cases.title: Summarize business documents
use_cases.description: Parse DOCX reports and generate AI-powered summaries for quick review of contracts, proposals, or meeting notes.

use_cases.title: Batch convert spreadsheets and documents
use_cases.description: Convert XLSX, HTML, and other supported formats into markdown for use in documentation pipelines or static site generators.

prompt_templates.title: Basic file to markdown conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse the file at ./report.pdf and save the markdown output to .firecrawl/report.md
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner — converting a single local file

prompt_templates.title: AI summary of a document
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse ./quarterly-report.docx with the AI summary flag enabled, saving to .firecrawl/q1-summary.md

prompt_templates.title: Question answering over a parsed file
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse ./contract.pdf and answer: 'What are the termination clauses?' Save the Q&A to .firecrawl/contract-qa.md

prompt_templates.title: Batch parsing multiple files
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse all DOCX files in ./invoices/ and save each as markdown in .firecrawl/. Use parallel execution where possible.

output: Creates the .firecrawl/ directory if it does not exist.
Converts the PDF to clean markdown without filling the context window.
Output saved to .firecrawl/paper.md for incremental reading with head, grep, or rg.
Generates an AI-powered summary of the document content.
Saves the summary as markdown to the specified output path.
Useful for quick review of long business documents.

output_examples.input: Parse ./manual.pdf -Q 'How do I reset the device?' -o .firecrawl/manual-qa.md
output: Extracts the answer to the specific question from the parsed content.
Saves the answer with relevant context to a markdown file.
Useful for technical support documentation and knowledge bases.

best_practices: Always use the `-o` flag to save parsed output to `.firecrawl/` instead of streaming to stdout.
Add `.firecrawl/` to your `.gitignore` to prevent committing parsed output files.
Use `head`, `grep`, or `rg` to incrementally read large parsed files rather than loading them entirely.
anti_patterns: Do not skip the `-o` flag and stream large documents to stdout; this can fill the context window.
Do not use this skill for URLs — use the `firecrawl-scrape` skill instead.
Do not attempt to parse files larger than 50 MB; split or preprocess them first.

faq.question: What file formats does firecrawl-parse support?
faq.answer: It supports PDF, DOCX, DOC, ODT, RTF, XLSX, XLS, and HTML/HTM/XHTML files.

faq.question: How do I avoid filling my context window with large documents?
faq.answer: Always use the `-o` flag to save parsed output to disk in the `.firecrawl/` directory, then read the file incrementally with head, grep, or rg.

faq.question: Can this skill scrape URLs?
faq.answer: No, this skill is for local files only. Use the `firecrawl-scrape` skill for URLs.

faq.question: What is the maximum file size?
faq.answer: The maximum upload size is 50 MB per file.

faq.question: How many credits does parsing consume?
faq.answer: PDFs cost approximately 1 credit per page. HTML files cost 1 credit flat regardless of size.

faq.question: How do I generate an AI summary of a document?
faq.answer: Use the `-S` or `--summary` flag along with the `-o` output flag to save the AI-generated summary.
